languages.text: "Ну вам, может, и не сложно. У вас-то там, наверное, есть заряжающие штуки, а вот у нас таких нет. Зато можно подкинуть батарейку в костер. Какое-то даже название есть правильное у этого ритуала, не вспомню. Проблема в том, что батарея и взорваться может. Да рисков такой обряд, все равно портит их. Так что те, кто знают, где правильно положить в костре и сколько там ее держать — они очень ценные бойцы".

traits.locale_text: "Ну вам, может, и не сложно. У вас-то там, наверное, есть заряжающие штуки, а вот у нас таких нет. Зато можно подкинуть батарейку в костер. Какое-то даже название есть правильное у этого ритуала, не вспомню. Проблема в том, что батарея и взорваться может. Да рисков такой обряд, все равно портит их. Так что те, кто знают, где правильно положить в костре и сколько там ее держать — они очень ценные бойцы".

languages.text: "Well, maybe it's easy for you. You probably have some charging things back where you're from, but we don't have anything like that. But you can throw a battery on a fire. There's even a proper name for the ritual, but I can't remember it. The problem is that sometimes the battery just explodes. It's risky, and the rite ruins them eventually anyway. So, the ones who know exactly where to put it in the fire and how long to let it burn, they're valuable soldiers."

Какое-то даже название есть правильное у этого ритуала, не вспомню. Проблема в том, что батарея и взорваться может. Да рисков такой обряд, все равно портит их. Так что те, кто знают, где правильно положить в костре и сколько там ее держать — они очень ценные бойцы".

traits.locale_text: ""Well, maybe it's easy for you. You probably have those 'charging thingies' back where you're from, but we don't have anything like that. But you can throw a battery on a fire. There's even a proper name for the ritual, but I can't remember it. The problem is that sometimes the battery just explodes. It's risky, and the rite ruins them eventually anyway. So the ones who know exactly where to put it in the fire and how long to let it burn, they're priceless soldiers."

"Well, maybe it's easy for you. You probably have those charging thingies back where you're from, but we don't have anything like that. But you can throw a battery on a fire. There's even a proper name for the ritual, but I can't remember it. The problem is that sometimes the battery just explodes. It's risky, and the rite ruins them eventually anyway. So, the ones who know exactly where to put it in the fire and how long to let it burn, they're valuable soldiers."

"If you have a source of machine spark, it's easy — but we don't. But you can throw the lasbattery on a fire. There's even a proper name for the ritual, but I can't remember it. The problem is that sometimes the battery recharges, and sometimes it just explodes. So the ones who know the right distance to throw it from and how long to let it burn, they're priceless experts."

So the ones who know exactly where to put it in the fire and how long to let it burn, they're priceless soldiers."

languages.text: 「機械の火花があれば簡単だけど、我々は持ってない。でも、火に放り込めばいい。儀式には名前もあるけど、覚えてない。問題は、充電される時と爆発する時がある。だから、適切な距離を投げたり、燃焼時間を決める人は、かけがえのない専門家なんだ」
languages.original_text: "If you have a source of machine spark, it's easy — but we don't. But you can throw the lasbattery on a fire. There's even a proper name for the ritual, but I can't remember it. The problem is that sometimes the battery recharges, and sometimes it just explodes. So the ones who know the right distance to throw it from and how long to let it burn, they're priceless experts."

languages.text: "Eh, belki sizin için kolaydır. Geldiğiniz yerde muhtemelen şarj zımbırtılarından vardır ama bizim öyle bir şeyimiz yok. Fakat ateşe batarya atabilirsiniz. Ayinin düzgün bir adı bile var ama hatırlayamıyorum. Sorun şu ki batarya bazen öylece patlayabiliyor. Riskli bir iş ve ayin, eninde sonunda onları mahvediyor zaten. Bu yüzden ateşte tam olarak nereye konulacağını ve ne kadar süre yanması gerektiğini bilenler... İşte onlar değerli askerlerdir."
languages.original_text: "Well, maybe it's easy for you. You probably have some charging things back where you're from, but we don't have anything like that. But you can throw a battery on a fire. There's even a proper name for the ritual, but I can't remember it. The problem is that sometimes the battery just explodes. It's risky, and the rite ruins them eventually anyway. So, the ones who know exactly where to put it in the fire and how long to let it burn, they're valuable soldiers."

Ayinin düzgün bir adı bile var ama hatırlayamıyorum. Sorun şu ki batarya bazen öylece patlayabiliyor. Riskli bir iş ve ayin, eninde sonunda onları mahvediyor zaten. Bu yüzden ateşte tam olarak nereye konulacağını ve ne kadar süre yanması gerektiğini bilenler... İşte onlar değerli askerlerdir."

"Eh, belki sizin için kolaydır. Geldiğiniz yerde muhtemelen o şarj zımbırtılarından vardır ama bizim öyle bir şeyimiz yok. Fakat ateşe batarya atabilirsiniz. Ayinin düzgün bir adı bile var ama hatırlayamıyorum. Sorun şu ki batarya bazen öylece patlayabiliyor. Riskli bir iş ve ayin, eninde sonunda onları mahvediyor zaten. Bu yüzden ateşte tam olarak nereye konulacağını ve ne kadar süre yanması gerektiğini bilenler... İşte onlar değerli askerlerdir."

languages.text: “嗯，对你们来说可能很简单。你们那边大概有些可以用来充电的东西，可我们这儿没有。不过，可以把电池扔到火里充电。这个仪式甚至有个正经名字，但我记不住了。问题在于，有时候那电池直接就炸了。这种办法风险挺大，而且这么搞迟早会把电池弄坏。所以，那些知道该把电池搁在火里的哪个位置、该烧多久的士兵，就是价值连城的专家。”
languages.original_text: "Well, maybe it's easy for you. You probably have some charging things back where you're from, but we don't have anything like that. But you can throw a battery on a fire. There's even a proper name for the ritual, but I can't remember it. The problem is that sometimes the battery just explodes. It's risky, and the rite ruins them eventually anyway. So, the ones who know exactly where to put it in the fire and how long to let it burn, they're valuable soldiers."

languages.text: "Tja, für Euch ist es vielleicht einfach. Wo Ihr herkommt, habt Ihr vermutlich diese Ladeteile, aber hier gibt es so was nicht. Aber man kann eine Batterie in ein Feuer werfen. Es gibt sogar einen Namen für das Ritual, aber an den erinnere ich mich nicht. Das Problem ist, dass die Batterie manchmal einfach nur explodiert. Das Ganze ist riskant und der Ritus ruiniert sie irgendwann sowieso. Wer genau weiß, wo eine Batterie im Feuer landen und wie lange sie brennen muss, gilt hier als wertvoller Soldat."
languages.original_text: "Well, maybe it's easy for you. You probably have some charging things back where you're from, but we don't have anything like that. But you can throw a battery on a fire. There's even a proper name for the ritual, but I can't remember it. The problem is that sometimes the battery just explodes. It's risky, and the rite ruins them eventually anyway. So, the ones who know exactly where to put it in the fire and how long to let it burn, they're valuable soldiers."

languages.text: « Pour vous, ce n'est sûrement rien de compliqué, mais ici, nous n'avons pas ces espèces de chargeurs dont vous vous servez certainement. Par contre, on peut jeter une batterie dans un feu, rituel dont le nom m'échappe. Le problème, c'est que parfois, la batterie se contente d'exploser. C'est une méthode risquée qui finit par les abîmer, de toute façon. Donc ceux qui savent où placer la batterie dans le feu et combien de temps la laisser brûler sont des soldats indispensables. »
languages.original_text: "Well, maybe it's easy for you. You probably have some charging things back where you're from, but we don't have anything like that. But you can throw a battery on a fire. There's even a proper name for the ritual, but I can't remember it. The problem is that sometimes the battery just explodes. It's risky, and the rite ruins them eventually anyway. So, the ones who know exactly where to put it in the fire and how long to let it burn, they're valuable soldiers."

« Pour vous, ce n'est sûrement rien de compliqué, mais ici, nous n'avons pas ces espèces de chargeurs dont vous vous servez certainement. Par contre, on peut jeter une batterie dans un feu, rituel dont le nom m'échappe. Le problème, c'est que parfois, la batterie se contente d'exploser. C'est une méthode risquée qui finit par les abîmer, de toute façon. Donc ceux qui savent où placer la batterie dans le feu et combien de temps la laisser brûler sont des soldats indispensables. »

languages.text: «A lo mejor es fácil para usted. Seguro que en su nave tienen artilugios de esos que se cargan, pero aquí no hay nada parecido. Sin embargo, se puede echar la batería láser al fuego. El ritual tiene nombre y todo, pero no lo recuerdo. El problema es que, a veces, en vez de recargarse, la batería explota. Es arriesgado y el ritual acaba arruinándolas todas tarde o temprano. Por eso, la gente que sabe en qué parte echarla y cuánto tiempo dejarla al fuego son expertos de valor incalculable».

So, the ones who know exactly where to put it in the fire and how long to let it burn, they're valuable soldiers."
